request.question: What was the record for the head football coach of this private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States?

title: Tim McCarty
Kansas Wesleyan University
Saint Joseph Seminary College
Willie Fritz
Ron Dupree
C. L. Williams
Coach Miller
Mark Raymond
Carl Anderson (American football)
Tom Keele
sentences: Tim McCarty is an American football coach and former player.
He is currently the head football coach at East Central University in Ada, Oklahoma, a position he first held from 2004 to 2005 and then resume in 2009.
From 1999 to 2003 McCarty was the head football coach at Tabor College in Hillsboro, Kansas.
His two tenures at East Central were sepeated by a three-year stint at the assistant head football coach at Kansas State University.
Kansas Wesleyan University is a private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States in 1886.
It is affiliated with the United Methodist Church.
About 850 students attend the 28 acre campus.

The university is accredited by the North Central Association of Colleges and Schools, the National League for Nursing, the National Council for Accreditation of Teacher Education (NCATE), and the Kansas State Department of Education.
Saint Joseph Seminary College, also known as St. Ben or St. Ben's, is a private four-year seminary college founded in 1891.
It is operated by the Benedictine Monks of Saint Joseph Abbey and the dioceses of the ecclesiastical provinces of New Orleans and Mobile, Alabama.
The 1200 acre piney woods campus is located in St. Benedict, Louisiana, north of Covington.
Willie Fritz (born April 2, 1960) is an American football coach and former player.
He is the current head coach at Tulane University.
From 2014 to 2015, he was head coach at Georgia Southern University.
From 2010 to 2013, he was the head football coach at Sam Houston State University.
From 1997 to 2009, Fritz served as the head football coach at the University of Central Missouri.
From 1993 to 1996, he was the head football coach at Blinn College, a junior college in Brenham, Texas.
Ron Dupree is a former American football coach.
Dupree was the 15th head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for three seasons, from 1979 to 1980 and again in 1996.
His coaching record at Kansas Wesleyan was 11–18.
C. L. Williams was an American football coach.
He was the third head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for two seasons, from 1908 to 1909, compiling a record of 9–5.
Coach Miller was an American football coach.
He was the second head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for one season, in 1906, and compiling a record of 0–1.
Mark Raymond is an American football coach, currently serving as the head football coach at Williams College.
Raymond served as the head football coach at St. Lawrence University from 2010 to 2015, winning two conference titles and compiling an overall record of 31–30.
Raymond was named Liberty League Coach of the Year following the conclusion of both the 2010 and 2015 seasons at St. Lawrence.
Raymond was named head football coach at Williams College on February 24, 2016.
Carl Rudolph Frederick "Swede" Anderson IV (September 9, 1898 – April 30, 1978) was an American college football coach at Western Kentucky University and Howard Payne University.
Anderson graduated from Centre College in Danville, Kentucky in 1924, where he played in the backfield with legendary alumnus Bo McMillin.
Anderson then followed McMillin to Centenary College of Louisiana and Geneva College.
Anderson then served one year as the head football coach at Western Kentucky, before moving to Kansas State as its freshman team coach in 1930.
Anderson returned to Western Kentucky as its head coach from 1934 to 1937.
He was the backfield coach under McMillin at Indiana from 1938 to 1945.
He then returned to his alma mater, Centre College, where he coached the Praying Colonels until 1950.
The following season, Anderson became the seventh head football coach at the Howard Payne University in Brownwood, Texas and held that position from 1951 to 1952.
His coaching record at Howard Payne was 7–10.
Tom Keele (born c. 1933) is a former American football coach.
He served as the head football coach at California State University, Northridge from 1979 to 1985, compiling a record of 31–42–1.
Keele graduated from Jefferson High School in Portland Oregon in 1951.
He attended the University of Oregon, where he played football for the Oregon Webfoots as a tackle from 1957 to 1959.
Keele began his coaching career in 1960 at North Eugene High School in Eugene, Oregon, working two years as an assistant football coach and sophomore basketball coach.
He moved to Oregon City High School in Oregon City, Oregon in 1962, serving as head football coach and leading his team to a 9–1–1 record.
The following year, he was hired as head football coach at the newly-formed Sheldon High School in Eugene.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What was the record for the head football coach of this private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States?
hop1_docs: Kansas Wesleyan University | Kansas Wesleyan University is a private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States in 1886.  It is affiliated with the United Methodist Church.  About 850 students attend the 28 acre campus.  The university is accredited by the North Central Association of Colleges and Schools, the National League for Nursing, the National Council for Accreditation of Teacher Education (NCATE), and the Kansas State Department of Education.
Bethel College (Kansas) | Bethel College is a four-year private Christian liberal arts college in North Newton, Kansas, United States.  It is affiliated with the Mennonite Church USA.

Coach Miller | Coach Miller was an American football coach.  He was the second head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for one season, in 1906, and compiling a record of 0–1.

1908 Kansas State Aggies football team | The 1908 Kansas State Aggies football team represented Kansas State Agricultural College (now Kansas State University) in the 1908 college football season.  In their fourth year under head coach Mike Ahearn, the Aggies compiled a 6–2 record, and outscored their opponents by a combined total of 182 to 74.

1909 Kansas State Aggies football team | The 1909 Kansas State Aggies football team represented Kansas State Agricultural College (now Kansas State University) in the 1909 college football season.  In their fifth year under head coach Mike Ahearn, the Aggies compiled a 7–2 record, and outscored their opponents by a combined total of 320 to 11.

Matt Drinkall | Matt Drinkall is an American football coach.  In January 2014 he was hired as the head coach of the Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes in Salina, Kansas.  He has worked previously as a football coach at the high school and college level.

W. F. Ragle | W. F. Ragle was an American football coach.  He was the fifth head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for one season, in 1915 and compiling a record of 5–4.
response.summary_1: The college is Kansas Wesleyan University ("Kansas Wesleyan University is a private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States"). Passages show two historical KU head coaches with records: "Coach Miller ... compiling a record of 0–1" and "W. F. Ragle ... compiling a record of 5–4." They also state "Matt Drinkall ... was hired as the head coach of the Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes in Salina, Kansas," but provide no win–loss record for him. Missing/next hop: retrieve the head coach record for Matt Drinkall (search terms: "Matt Drinkall record" or "Matt Drinkall Kansas Wesleyan record") or disambiguate which named coach's record the question intends.
hop2_docs: Matt Drinkall | Matt Drinkall is an American football coach.  In January 2014 he was hired as the head coach of the Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes in Salina, Kansas.  He has worked previously as a football coach at the high school and college level.

Ron Dupree | Ron Dupree is a former American football coach.  Dupree was the 15th head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for three seasons, from 1979 to 1980 and again in 1996.  His coaching record at Kansas Wesleyan was 11–18.

Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes | The Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes are the athletic teams that represent Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas.  They are part of the National Association of Intercollegiate Athletics (NAIA), competing in the Kansas Collegiate Athletic Conference (KCAC).

W. F. Ragle | W. F. Ragle was an American football coach.  He was the fifth head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for one season, in 1915 and compiling a record of 5–4.

Zora G. Clevenger | Zora G. Clevenger (December 12, 1881 – November 24, 1970) was an American football, basketball, and baseball player, coach, and pioneering athletic director.  He served as the head football coach at Nebraska Wesleyan University (1908–1910), the University of Tennessee (1911–1915), and Kansas State University (1916–1919), compiling a record of 47–32–7.  Clevenger was also the head basketball coach at Indiana University (1904–1906), Nebraska Wesleyan (1907–1911), Tennessee (1911–1916), and Kansas State (1916–1919), and was baseball coach at Indiana (1905–1906), Nebraska Wesleyan (1908–1911), Tennessee (1911–1916), and Kansas State (1919–1921).  Clevenger served as the athletic director at Kansas State (1916–1920), the University of Missouri (1921–1923), and Indiana (1923–1946).  He was inducted into the College Football Hall of Fame as a player in 1968.

C. L. Williams | C. L. Williams was an American football coach.  He was the third head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for two seasons, from 1908 to 1909, compiling a record of 9–5.

Coach Miller | Coach Miller was an American football coach.  He was the second head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for one season, in 1906, and compiling a record of 0–1.

Williams 9–5 in 1908–1909; Ron Dupree 11–18 for his Kansas Wesleyan tenure), and note Matt Drinkall was hired in 2014 but give no win–loss record for him, so it’s unclear which single "head coach" record the question intends.
